MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, S. Philip Martin, Esq. (415.794.1856) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Andrew Castricone OF gORDON & rEES (415.875.3183) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

DEFENDANTS HELENE WASSERMAN AND RALPHS GROCERY COMPANY'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To 42 U.S.C. 1983, 1988 is GRANTED commencing in January of 2013 based on finding of frivolous maintenance of lawsuit. The sole basis for the determination of frivolousness is that no reasonable attorney or litigant could believe that there was, even assuming a violation of section 1983, or if there is such a thing as a claim of conspiracy to violate section 1983, even assuming a substantive violation, that violation was not the cause, as the term "cause" is used in 1983 case law, of the deprivation of the federal right of Mr. Kay. To the extent that this order is not automatically stayed upon filing of an appeal, the Court exercises its discretion to stay the matter between now and the filing of a notice of appeal. There should be no collection activity of any kind until the First District has ruled. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
